name: security-toolkit
description: Useful friction against obvious mistakes — a basic draft, not a security product (no sandbox; whatever doesn't match a pattern passes through). Threat-detection and dangerous-action-blocking hooks for Claude Code: prompt-injection detection (tiered, with allowlist; covers MCP tool outputs via the * matcher), block dangerous git operations (push to main, force push, reset --hard, --no-verify, etc.), and Desktop Commander input safeguards (block start_process and set_config_value). Also ships an optional, per-user PR-merge guard — block Claude from running `gh pr merge` so a human always merges — that is OFF by default and toggled with the `/pr-merge-guard` command (on/off/status); the `pr-merge-guard` skill explains it and flips it on request. New: the `windows-wsl-security-verification` skill — a guided "am I compromised?" IOC triage + attack-surface reduction for Windows + WSL2 dev boxes after a supply-chain scare (poisoned npm/PyPI package, trojaned extension), with the detection-reading discriminators that prevent false alarms. Windows/WSL-specific; verifies, does not harden; a clean result raises confidence, never proves.
